2017-04-25.abstract: The United States and South Korea began installing key elements of an advanced missile-defense system known as Thaad in a rural southern county on Wednesday.

2017-04-25.title: Genocide’s Legacy: A Reconciliation Village in Rwanda
2017-04-25.abstract: The 1994 Rwandan genocide tore the country apart. But today, in the village of Mbyo, perpetrators and victims live side by side.

2017-04-25.title: How a Nation Reconciles After Genocide Killed Nearly a Million People
2017-04-25.abstract: “Reconciliation villages” place homes for those convicted of carrying out the violence alongside those who suffered at their hands.

2017-04-26.title: China, Sending a Signal, Launches a Home-Built Aircraft Carrier
2017-04-26.abstract: The ship is far from ready for operations, but it was hailed as a milestone in President Xi Jinping’s drive to extend China’s military reach far beyond its shores.

2017-04-26.title: As Rising Seas Erode Shorelines, Tasmania Shows What Can Be Lost
2017-04-26.abstract: Port Arthur, the famous prison that housed the British Empire’s toughest convicts in the 19th century, is facing an onslaught from the sea.

2017-04-26.title: Fearing Korean Nuclear War, Women of 40 Nations Urge Trump to Seek Peace
2017-04-26.abstract: Female activists from North and South Korea, and many other countries, wrote to America’s president, saying: “Diplomacy is the only way to resolve the nuclear crisis.”

2017-04-26.title: Genocide’s Legacy: Preserving Auschwitz
2017-04-26.abstract: Work is underway at Nazi Germany’s largest death camp to preserve the memory of the Holocaust.

2017-04-26.title: On the Mosul Front, a Brutal Battle Against ISIS and Time
2017-04-26.abstract: Times reporters witnessed the grinding, block-by-block fight for western Mosul, where civilians are trapped near Islamic State vehicle bombs and snipers.

2017-04-27.title: Kevin O’Leary, Reality TV Star, Ends Bid to Lead Canada’s Conservatives
2017-04-27.abstract: Mr. O’Leary said his failure to attract enough support in Quebec undermined his ability to lead the Conservatives to victory over the Liberal Party of Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.

2017-04-27.title: The Drumbeats Don’t Add Up to Imminent War With North Korea
2017-04-27.abstract: President Trump views North Korea as his chief foreign policy issue, but he says he is pursuing a strategy that relies primarily on help from China.

2017-04-27.title: He Tweeted About Chinese Government Corruption. Twitter Suspended His Account.
2017-04-27.abstract: Guo Wengui, an outspoken Chinese-born billionaire whom China views as a criminal, has alarmed Beijing with his public denunciations.

2017-04-27.title: Venezuela Says It Will Leave Pro-Democracy Organization
2017-04-27.abstract: The country plans to pull out of the Organization of American States, which has long criticized President Nicolás Maduro’s unyielding accumulation of power.

2017-04-27.title: Far-Right Leaders Loathe the European Parliament, but Love Its Paychecks
2017-04-27.abstract: For right-wing lawmakers who attack the European Union, a perch in Parliament can mean a lucrative sinecure, easy news media exposure and immunity from criminal prosecution at home.

2017-04-28.title: Facing Tough Odds in French Vote, Le Pen Assails Macron
2017-04-28.abstract: At a rally in Nice the far-right presidential candidate Marine Le Pen depicted her centrist opponent, Emmanuel Macron, as an unpatriotic out-of-touch banker.

2017-04-28.title: Zoran Zaev, Macedonian Lawmaker, Is Bloodied in Attack on Parliament by Nationalists
2017-04-28.abstract: The demonstrators, who threw chairs, wounded three other lawmakers and attacked journalists, were protesting the election of a new speaker.

2017-04-28.title: Trump Warns That ‘Major, Major Conflict’ With North Korea Is Possible
2017-04-28.abstract: He praised the Chinese president for efforts to resolve the dispute with the North but cautioned that such diplomatic efforts might fail.

2017-04-28.title: At Home With Omar and Sarah, a Syrian Refugee Story
2017-04-28.abstract: The Times spent months with several Syrian refugee families as they navigated integration into Weimar, Germany. In 360, step inside the home of Sarah, a German, and Omar, a refugee, to see how they are adjusting.

2017-04-28.title: A Hidden Church in Cairo Pins Its Hopes on Good Will From the Pope’s Visit
2017-04-28.abstract: The small Coptic Catholic church’s battle to obtain a license highlights the enduring discrimination against Christians codified in Egyptian law.

2017-04-29.title: Trump’s 100 Days on World Stage: Rallying Some and Repelling Others
2017-04-29.abstract: The new presidency has reverberated worldwide, fueling some far-right groups, weakening others and stirring doubts about America’s status as a global exemplar.

2017-04-29.title: Shobha Nehru Dies at 108; Escaped Holocaust and Married Into Indian Politics
2017-04-29.abstract: After marrying B.K. Nehru in 1935 and moving to India, Mrs. Nehru saw religious and ethnic violence convulse both her native and adopted countries.

2017-04-29.title: In Mexico, ‘It’s Easy to Kill a Journalist’
2017-04-29.abstract: The state of Veracruz is the hemisphere’s most dangerous place to be a reporter. The authorities seem ill equipped to stop attacks on the press, and in some cases appear entangled in them.

2017-04-29.title: Despite the ‘Yuck Factor,’ Leeches Are Big in Russian Medicine
2017-04-29.abstract: Russians use about 10 million leeches a year to treat heart disease, glaucoma, prostatitis, hypertension and even lower back pain.

2017-04-29.title: China Deports American Woman Convicted on Spying Charge
2017-04-29.abstract: The deportation ended uncertainty about the fate of Phan Phan-Gillis of Houston, who was sentenced on Tuesday to three and a half years in prison on an espionage charge.

2017-04-30.title: Tony Blair Urges U.K. to Stay Centered, and Close to Europe
2017-04-30.abstract: Twenty years after his landslide election as prime minister, Mr. Blair says he will “never give up” on the idea of remaining in the European Union.

2017-04-30.title: Trump Invites Rodrigo Duterte to the White House
2017-04-30.abstract: The two leaders spoke late on Saturday and addressed the Philippines’ violent campaign against drugs, which has been condemned by human rights groups and many Western governments.

2017-04-30.title: U.S. Confirms It Will Pay for Antimissile System, South Korea Says
2017-04-30.abstract: President Trump said last week that South Korea should pay for the so-called Thaad system, but officials said Sunday that the original agreement would not change.

2017-04-30.title: China’s Appetite Pushes Fisheries to the Brink
2017-04-30.abstract: Overfishing is depleting oceans across the globe, but China’s enormous appetite and massive fleet of deep-sea fishing vessels is having an outsize impact.

2017-04-30.title: Young, Idealistic but Determined, Slovaks Lead Anticorruption Crusade
2017-04-30.abstract: With anti-authoritarian protests erupting across Eastern Europe, a pair of teenagers are among the young people taking action.
